candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above operating profit, but it is a company-level operating D&A total and not identified as COGS-specific; adding it to COGS would risk misallocation and double counting across operating buckets.

Component of the total D&A line, not labelled as COGS-specific; should not be assigned to COGS without explicit bucket linkage.

variables.notes: A separate operating D&A line exists at the same level as other operating rows, so D&A does not appear embedded in the selected COGS row alone. However, the excerpt provides no COGS-specific D&A split, so no row can be safely added specifically to COGS without arbitrary allocation.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above operating profit and separate from the selected XSGA component rows, but it is a total operating D&A line rather than SG&A-specific; adding it wholly to XSGA would likely overstate SG&A D&A.
Component of total D&A, not labelled as SG&A-specific; inferior to the summary row and not safely attributable to XSGA only.
Component of total D&A, not labelled as SG&A-specific; cannot be confidently mapped just to XSGA.

variables.notes: Manually added. The selected XSGA bucket consists of 'Payroll and related costs' and 'Other operating expenses'. The statement shows a separate operating D&A line outside those rows, so D&A is likely not embedded in the chosen XSGA rows. But the available rows do not split D&A between COGS and XSGA, and XRD is empty, so assigning the total D&A row wholly to XSGA would be unsafe. Conservative output is no separate add-back pending manual allocation.
